title: Fix admin panel navigation button not appearing after authentication

requirements.summary: Debug and fix the Layout component to ensure the Admin navigation button renders immediately after authentication
acceptanceCriteria: Admin navigation button appears in the Layout header when an authenticated user logs in
Button visibility is not delayed or blocked by conditional rendering logic
Authentication state changes trigger proper re-renders of the Layout component

file_operations.description: Add comprehensive debug logging to trace authentication state, useIsOwner return values, and conditional rendering logic. Log identity presence, isOwner value, loading states, and button visibility conditions. Ensure the admin button rendering logic properly handles all authentication states and re-renders when authentication completes.

requirements.summary: Verify and fix the useIsOwner hook to handle authentication state changes without rendering delays
acceptanceCriteria: useIsOwner hook responds immediately to authentication state changes
Hook returns correct admin status for the email jj7250328@gmail.com
No loading states block the Layout component from rendering the Admin button

file_operations.description: Add detailed console logging for authentication state transitions, actor availability, query execution timing, and isCallerAdmin backend responses. Log the exact email comparison logic and return values. Ensure the hook's enabled condition properly tracks actor readiness and refetches when authentication state changes. Add timestamp logging to identify timing delays between authentication and admin status resolution.

requirements.summary: Add comprehensive frontend console logging across authentication flow components
acceptanceCriteria: Console logs show authentication state transitions
Logs display useIsOwner hook return values and types
Logs confirm whether conditional rendering logic evaluates to show or hide the button
Logs include timestamps to identify timing issues

file_operations.description: Add console logging with timestamps to track actor creation, authentication state changes, identity transitions, and query invalidation events. Log when the actor switches between anonymous and authenticated states to correlate with useIsOwner timing.

Add useEffect hook to log all relevant state changes including identity, isOwner, isLoading, and the final button visibility condition. Log component re-renders with timestamp and full state snapshot to identify when and why the button visibility changes.
